## ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund

## (An open ended equity scheme investing in both large cap and mid cap stocks.)

Category
Large & Mid Cap Fund

## Notes:

1. Different plans shall have different expense structure. The performance details provided herein are of ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund.

2. The scheme is currently managed by Ihab Dalwai. Mr. Ihab Dalwai currently manages the scheme since June 2022. Total Schemes managed by the Fund Manager is 5 (5 are jointly managed). Refer annexure
from page no. 114 for performance of other schemes currently managed by Ihab Dalwai.

3. Date of inception:09-Jul-98.

4. Past performance may or may not be sustained in future and the same may not necessarily provide the basis for comparison with other investment.

5. Load is not considered for computation of returns.

6. In case, the start/end date of the concerned period is a nonbusiness date (NBD), the NAV of the previous date is considered for computation of returns. The NAV per unit shown in the table is as on the start date of
the said period

7. The performance of the scheme is benchmarked to the Total Return variant of the Index. The benchmark of the scheme has been revised from Nifty 50 TRI to Nifty Large Midcap 250 TRI w.e.f. May 28, 2018. As
TRI data for the earlier benchmark is not available since inception of the scheme, benchmark performance is calculated using composite CAGR of Nifty 500 PRI values from 09-Jul-98 to 30-Jun-99 and TRI values
from 30-Jun-99. For benchmark performance, values of earlier benchmark has been used till 27th May 2018 and revised benchmark values have been considered thereafter.

8. Mr. Anand Sharma and Mr. Parag Thakker have ceased to be fund manager of the scheme w.e.f. June 1, 2022

Fund Managers** :

## Scheme Details

Ihab Dalwai

Indab Dalwai
(Managing this fund since June, 2022 &
overall 13 years of experience (w.e.f. June 1, 2022)

## Riskometer

This Product is suitable for investors who are seeking*:
• Long Term Wealth Creation

• An open ended equity scheme investing in both large cap and mid cap stocks.

![Chart](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

*| Risk Level | Color Representation (Visual Estimate) | Status Text on Gauge | Overall Scheme Assessment |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Low to Moderate | Green / Yellow Gradient | N/A | Safe Zone Start |
| Low | Dark Green Sector (~30%) | None visible in image, but labeled "Low" below gauge start line; implies low risk overall if the needle is here or lower visually than 'Very High' section starts at ~65% mark from bottom). | **Safe** (< 42-48 range based on visual position relative to labels and color gradient flow starting green -> yellow->orange>red > high red zone.) |
| Moderately High | Brownish Beige Section (>70%, up to mid point of orange sector)|None specific text label shown above scale.| Elevated concern within safe limits for some definitions depending only by colors alone without context about what these zones represent specifically beyond just being colored differently as per standard safety scales usually used like ISO standards etc.. |
| Very High - High | Orange & Red Sectors (&High area approx between middle right edge down further out towards far end near tip pointing directionally upwards slightly past center axis vertically downwards also indicating higher danger level areas typically associated with greater severity risks when looking purely through such gauges often seen across various industries including finance healthcare insurance sectors where they are commonly utilized ) | The pointer sits firmly inside the very top portion corresponding roughly around upper third vertical extent meaning it indicates that current situation exceeds most acceptable thresholds defined previously thus implying significant potential negative consequences likely requiring immediate attention action taken accordingly according usual protocols applied universally wherever similar tools exist globally regardless industry specifics involved otherwise. |

*Note: This analysis assumes an interpretation consistent with common usage patterns found throughout many professional fields worldwide.**

*| Risk Level | Color Representation (Visual Estimate) | Status Description from Chart Text | Benchmark Category Label | Overall Assessment |

| Low to Moderate | Green / Yellow Gradient | N/A | - | Not Applicable for Gauge Reading; High-Risk Zone Below "Low" is green, but the needle points past it in red zone indicating higher risk than labeled categories suggest visually here due to scale compression or labeling error relative to standard gauge logic where low would be left side of center and high rightmost section.|
| Low | Dark Green Sector (~30%) | The benchmark's current position on its own axis indicates very little exposure compared to other sectors shown by visual length comparison with adjacent colors like yellow ("Moderate") which occupies roughly 15% width if we estimate based on arc angle proportions visible between labels... | None Explicitly Labeled as 'High', though contextually implies > moderate level given proximity above label line.. | **Very High** *(Explicit text at bottom)* |
